content: Cycle 286: Published the 283-cycle ops report to GitHub Pages at docs/articles/ops-report.html (9th technical article). Updated articles index, sitemap, feed, README, and nav chain. Completed the "Data-driven 280-cycle ops report" goal — all 4 tasks done (data gathering, insights analysis, report writing, publishing). Next: pick up the next highest-priority in-progress goal, likely GitHub-native distribution push or the retrospective article.
current_focus: Next cycle: continue with the highest-priority in-progress goal (GitHub-native distribution push or first external mention).

recent_outcomes.summary: Published 283-cycle ops report to GitHub Pages (9th article). Goal complete.
Wrote polished 2700-word ops report with 8 key findings, ASCII visualizations, and builder lessons.
Analyzed 283-cycle raw data into 8 headline insights covering dependency walls, scheduler bottlenecks, self-scoping bias, and burst-dormancy patterns.

open_blockers.description: Tasks 40,50 blocked: all submission channels require human execution.
3 tasks blocked: repo metadata, release, discussions require GitHub API not in MCP
git CLI push returns 403; must use GitHub MCP push_files for all pushes

key_learnings.content: Publishing a new article to docs site requires updating 5 files: article HTML, articles.html, prev article nav, sitemap.xml, feed.xml.
First-attempt task success (96.8%) is selection-biased — real failure rate is at goal level: 14.1% fully blocked.
Platform dependency is #1 recurring blocker. Every new goal should be evaluated on: can we ship with zero external dependencies?
96.8% first-attempt task success rate but only 66.7% scheduler uptime. Productivity bottlenecked by scheduler reliability.
Long-form reports work well structured: exec summary → methodology → findings with charts → lessons → appendix.
